message.content:
Research depthai_ros_driver (ros-humble-depthai, version ~2.29.0) ROS 2 parameters that affect STABILITY and AUTO-RECOVERY of an OAK-D-LITE on a Raspberry Pi 4.

Use WebSearch and WebFetch (load via ToolSearch "select:WebSearch,WebFetch"). Search the luxonis/depthai-ros GitHub repo (README, ros2_examples, the parameter definitions in depthai_ros_driver), Luxonis docs.

Enumerate every relevant parameter with its purpose, type, default, and recommended value for a power-constrained Pi4:
- camera.i_usb_speed (HIGH vs SUPER vs SUPER_PLUS), camera.i_pipeline_type, camera.i_nn_type, camera.i_enable_imu, camera.i_reset_on_start, camera.i_restart_on_diagnostics_error (or similarly named auto-restart param), camera.i_mx_id / i_ip, camera.i_watchdog* / i_max_q_size.
- stereo/left/right/rgb: i_fps, i_resolution, i_low_bandwidth, i_publish_topic, i_max_q_size, i_set_isp_scale, output bandwidth knobs.
- Anything that makes the NODE auto-recover after a device disconnect (re-init the device) rather than sitting dead.

Return a markdown TABLE of params (name | purpose | default | recommended-for-Pi4 | why) plus prose on which combination gives the most stable + auto-recovering bring-up. Cite URLs.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Now let me search for the relevant documentation and parameter definitions.

input.query: depthai_ros_driver camera_node ROS 2 parameters i_pipeline_type i_usb_speed i_restart_on_diagnostics_error
luxonis depthai-ros camera.i_reset_on_start i_restart_on_diagnostics_error auto recover device disconnect

content.content:
Web search results for query: "depthai_ros_driver camera_node ROS 2 parameters i_pipeline_type i_usb_speed i_restart_on_diagnostics_error"

Links: [{"title":"GitHub - westonrobot/depthai_ros: Official ROS Driver for DepthAI Sensors. · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/westonrobot/depthai_ros"},{"title":"Setting up Luxonis Oak-D Lite stereo camera for ROS2 Jazzy on Ubuntu 24.04 on Raspberry Pi 5 – Personal Blog","url":"https://jessestevens.com.au/2025/05/10/setting-up-luxonis-oak-d-lite-stereo-camera-for-ros2-jazzy-on-ubuntu-24-04-on-raspberry-pi-5/"},{"title":"depthai-ros/depthai_ros_driver/src/camera.cpp at humble · luxonis/depthai-ros","url":"https://github.com/luxonis/depthai-ros/blob/humble/depthai_ros_driver/src/camera.cpp"},{"title":"DepthAI ROS Driver","url":"https://docs.luxonis.com/software-v3/depthai/ros/depthai-ros/driver"},{"title":"Using USB2 with dai ros2 driver not possible · Issue #317 · luxonis/depthai-ros","url":"https://github.com/luxonis/depthai-ros/issues/317"},{"title":"Driver Parameters","url":"https://docs.luxonis.com/software-v3/depthai/ros/parameters"},{"title":"Different intrinsic matrix values for rectified left and right lens · Issue #546 · luxonis/depthai-ros","url":"https://github.com/luxonis/depthai-ros/issues/546"},{"title":"ROS Driver | luxonis/depthai-ros | DeepWiki","url":"https://deepwiki.com/luxonis/depthai-ros/4-ros-driver"}]

Based on the search results, here's information about the depthai_ros_driver camera_node ROS 2 parameters you asked about:

## i_pipeline_type
This parameter sets the pipeline type, with default value RGBD. Valid options include 'Depth' and 'RGBD'.

## i_usb_speed
This parameter configures the USB speed, with default value SUPER_PLUS. Based on user discussions, valid options include HIGH and SUPER_SPEED.

## i_restart_on_diagnostics_error
To enable auto restart if camera encounters an error, set camera.i_restart_on_diagnostics_error: true. The default value is false, and when enabled, if diagnostics publishing is enabled in the pipeline and reports an error, the driver will be restarted.

## Parameter Naming Convention
Parameters in ROS2 are namespaced using "." (dot), while parameters in ROS1 are namespaced using "_" (underscore). Parameters that begin with i_ are set when camera is initializing, and to change them you have to call stop and start services.

Links: [{"title":"[BUG] Drop in pointcloud rate after running for a couple of hours · Issue #591 · luxonis/depthai-ros","url":"https://github.com/luxonis/depthai-ros/issues/591"},{"title":"OAK-D-PRO-W can not start directly with depthai_ros_driver - Luxonis Forum","url":"https://discuss.luxonis.com/d/6506-oak-d-pro-w-can-not-start-directly-with-depthai-ros-driver"},{"title":"DepthAI ROS Driver","url":"https://docs.luxonis.com/software-v3/depthai/ros/depthai-ros/driver"},{"title":"Different intrinsic matrix values for rectified left and right lens · Issue #546 · luxonis/depthai-ros","url":"https://github.com/luxonis/depthai-ros/issues/546"},{"title":"depthai-ros - ROS Package Overview","url":"https://index.ros.org/p/depthai-ros/"},{"title":"ROS 2 Jazzy depthai_ros_driver for oak-d-lite Non-responsive at 100% CPU After stop/start_camera · Issue #657 · luxonis/depthai-ros","url":"https://github.com/luxonis/depthai-ros/issues/657"},{"title":"Driver Parameters","url":"https://docs.luxonis.com/software-v3/depthai/ros/parameters"},{"title":"How To Diagnose depthai_ros_driver camera.launch.py issue? - Luxonis Forum","url":"https://discuss.luxonis.com/d/3624-how-to-diagnose-depthai-ros-driver-cameralaunchpy-issue"},{"title":"depthai-ros/depthai_ros_driver/src/camera.cpp at humble · luxonis/depthai-ros","url":"https://github.com/luxonis/depthai-ros/blob/humble/depthai_ros_driver/src/camera.cpp"}]

Based on the search results, I found relevant information about the depthai-ros camera parameters you're asking about:

## Key Parameters

**i_restart_on_diagnostics_error** (default: false): If diagnostics publishing is enabled in the pipeline and it reports an error, driver will be restarted.

This parameter enables automatic recovery when the camera encounters errors during operation. There is an option to enable auto restart if camera encounters an error by setting camera.i_restart_on_diagnostics_error: true.

## Usage Examples

In the search results, I can see examples of this parameter being used in real-world configurations:

- i_restart_on_diagnostics_error: true is used in multi-camera setups with OAK-D S2 cameras
- The parameter can be set to false when diagnostics auto-restart is not desired

However, I didn't find specific information about `i_reset_on_start` in these results. For more detailed information about that specific parameter and the complete auto-recovery behavior for device disconnects, you may want to check the official Luxonis documentation or GitHub repository directly.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: Let me fetch the canonical parameter documentation and the source-level parameter definitions in the humble branch of the repo.

input.prompt: List every camera-level (camera.i_*) and sensor-level (stereo/left/right/rgb i_*) parameter with its purpose, type, default value, and allowed options. Especially: i_usb_speed, i_pipeline_type, i_nn_type, i_enable_imu, i_reset_on_start, i_restart_on_diagnostics_error, i_mx_id, i_ip, i_max_q_size, i_fps, i_resolution, i_low_bandwidth, i_publish_topic, i_set_isp_scale, watchdog, any auto-recovery params. Reproduce the exact default values.

List every parameter declared (declareAndLogParam) with its exact default value. Especially i_usb_speed, i_pipeline_type, i_nn_type, i_enable_imu, i_reset_on_start, i_restart_on_diagnostics_error, i_mx_id, i_ip, i_pipeline_dump, i_calibration_dump, i_external_calibration_path, watchdog params, i_publish_tf_from_calibration.

content.content:
# Parameters Declared in camera_param_handler.cpp

Based on the source file, here are the parameters with their default values:

| Parameter | Default Value |
|-----------|----------------|
| `i_pipeline_type` | `"RGBD"` |
| `i_nn_type` | `"spatial"` |
| `i_enable_imu` | `true` |
| `i_enable_diagnostics` | `true` |
| `i_enable_sync` | `true` |
| `i_enable_ir` | `true` |
| `i_usb_speed` | `"SUPER"` |
| `i_mx_id` | `""` |
| `i_ip` | `""` |
| `i_usb_port_id` | `""` |
| `i_pipeline_dump` | `false` |
| `i_calibration_dump` | `false` |
| `i_external_calibration_path` | `""` |
| `i_laser_dot_brightness` | `800` (range: 0-1200) |
| `i_floodlight_brightness` | `0` (range: 0-1500) |
| `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error` | `false` |
| `i_rs_compat` | `false` |
| `i_publish_tf_from_calibration` | `false` |
| `i_tf_camera_name` | Node name |
| `i_tf_camera_model` | `""` |
| `i_tf_base_frame` | `"oak"` |
| `i_tf_parent_frame` | `"oak-d-base-frame"` |
| `i_tf_cam_pos_x/y/z` | `"0.0"` |
| `i_tf_cam_roll/pitch/yaw` | `"0.0"` |
| `i_tf_imu_from_descr` | `"false"` |
| `i_tf_custom_urdf_location` | `""` |
| `i_tf_custom_xacro_args` | `""` |

**Note:** Watchdog parameters are not declared in this file.

# DepthAI ROS Driver Parameters Reference

## Camera-Level Parameters

### Driver Parameters
- **`i_usb_speed`** (string, default: `SUPER`)
  - Purpose: Sets USB interface speed
  - Options: `LOW`, `FULL`, `HIGH`, `SUPER`, `SUPER_PLUS`

- **`i_device_id`** (string, default: `""`)
  - Purpose: Connect to device with specific ID

- **`i_ip`** (string, default: `""`)
  - Purpose: Connect to device at specific IP address

- **`i_usb_port_id`** (string, default: `""`)
  - Purpose: Connect to devices on specific USB port

- **`i_pipeline_dump`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Dump created pipeline to JSON for debugging

- **`i_calibration_dump`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Export device calibration to JSON

- **`i_external_calibration_path`** (string, default: `""`)
  - Purpose: Override device calibration with custom file

- **`i_pipeline_auto_calibration_mode`** (string/bool, default: `""`)
  - Purpose: "Pipeline auto-calibration mode"
  - Options: `OFF`, `ON_START`, `CONTINUOUS`; boolean legacy support

- **`i_enable_ir`** (bool, default: `true`)
  - Purpose: Enable IR projector

- **`i_restart_on_diagnostics_error`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Restart driver if diagnostics reports error

- **`i_rs_compat`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Enable RealSense compatibility mode for topic/frame names

- **`r_laser_dot_intensity`** (float, range: 0.0-1.0, default: `0.6`)
  - Purpose: Set laser dot projector intensity

- **`r_floodlight_intensity`** (float, range: 0.0-1.0, default: `0.6`)
  - Purpose: Set floodlight projector intensity

## Pipeline Generation Parameters

- **`i_pipeline_type`** (string, default: not specified)
  - Purpose: Define pipeline structure
  - Options: `RGB`, `RGBD`, `Stereo`, `RGBStereo`, `Depth`, `CamArray`, `Tof`, `DepthTof`, `StereoToF`, `RGBToF`, `Thermal`

- **`i_nn_type`** (string, default: `spatial`)
  - Purpose: "Type of NN to be created with pipelines"
  - Options: `none`, `rgb`, `spatial`

- **`i_enable_imu`** (bool, default: `true`)
  - Purpose: Enable IMU node in compatible pipelines

- **`i_enable_diagnostics`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Enable diagnostics node

- **`i_enable_rgbd`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Enable RGBD node in compatible pipelines

## Sensor-Level Parameters

### Initial Parameters
- **`i_max_q_size`** (int, default: `8`)
  - Purpose: Sets DAI queue size

- **`i_fps`** (float, default: `30.0`)
  - Purpose: Sensor frame rate

- **`i_low_bandwidth`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Enable video encoder for improved latency

- **`i_low_bandwidth_profile`** (int, default: `4`)
  - Purpose: Encoder type selection
  - Options: `H264_BASELINE`, `H264_HIGH`, `H264_MAIN`, `H265_MAIN`, `MJPEG`

- **`i_low_bandwidth_frame_freq`** (int, default: `30`)
  - Purpose: H26X encoder frame frequency

- **`i_low_bandwidth_bitrate`** (int, default: `0`)
  - Purpose: H26X encoder bitrate

- **`i_low_bandwidth_quality`** (int, default: `50`)
  - Purpose: MJPEG encoder quality level

- **`i_publish_compressed`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Publish encoded frames as compressed image formats

- **`i_get_base_device_timestamp`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Synchronize DAI timestamps to host

- **`i_calibration_file`** (string, default: `""`)
  - Purpose: Override device calibration with ROS calibration

- **`i_update_ros_base_time_on_ros_msg`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Correct for external system clock control

- **`i_enable_lazy_publisher`** (bool, default: `true`)
  - Purpose: Publish only when subscriptions detected

- **`i_add_exposure_offset`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Add exposure offset to ROS timestamp

- **`i_exposure_offset`** (int, default: `0`)
  - Purpose: Exposure offset timing
  - Options: `START`, `MIDDLE`, `END`

- **`i_reverse_stereo_socket_order`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Reverse stereo pair ordering

- **`i_synced`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Add output to synchronized group

- **`i_publish_topic`** (bool, default: `true` for CAM_A, `false` otherwise)
  - Purpose: Enable ROS topic publishing

- **`i_publish_full_resolution`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Publish at full sensor resolution

- **`i_use_max_resolution_possible`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Use maximum available resolution

- **`i_width`** (int, default: `640`)
  - Purpose: Image width in pixels

- **`i_height`** (int, default: `400`)
  - Purpose: Image height in pixels

- **`i_undistorted`** (bool, default: `true`)
  - Purpose: Apply distortion correction

- **`i_resize_mode`** (string, default: `CROP`)
  - Purpose: Resizing method for undistortion
  - Options: `CROP`, `LETTERBOX`, `STRETCH`

- **`i_fsync_continuous`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Enable frame synchronization mode

- **`i_fsync_mode`** (string, default: `INPUT`)
  - Purpose: Frame sync operating mode
  - Options: `OFF`, `OUTPUT`, `INPUT`

- **`i_fsync_trigger`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Use external trigger for capture

- **`i_num_frames_burst`** (int, default: `1`)
  - Purpose: Frames per triggered burst

- **`i_num_frames_discard`** (int, default: `0`)
  - Purpose: Initial frames to discard for quality

### Runtime Parameters
- **`r_iso`** (int, range: 100-1600, default: `800`)
  - Purpose: ISO for manual exposure mode

- **`r_exposure`** (int, range: 1-33000, default: `1000`)
  - Purpose: Exposure duration for manual mode

- **`r_set_man_exposure`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Activate manual exposure control

- **`r_set_autoexposure_limit`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Limit auto exposure range

- **`r_auto_exposure_limit`** (int, default: `1000`)
  - Purpose: Maximum auto exposure value

- **`r_set_man_focus`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Activate manual focus control

- **`r_focus`** (int, range: 1-255, default: `1`)
  - Purpose: Manual focus distance value

- **`r_set_man_whitebalance`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Activate manual white balance

- **`r_whitebalance`** (int, range: 1000-12000, default: `3000`)
  - Purpose: Manual white balance color temperature

- **`r_set_sharpness`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Control sharpness manually

- **`r_sharpness`** (int, range: 1-4, default: `1`)
  - Purpose: Sharpness enhancement level

- **`r_set_chroma_denoise`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Apply chroma denoising

- **`r_chroma_denoise`** (int, range: 1-4, default: `1`)
  - Purpose: Chroma noise reduction level

- **`r_set_luma_denoise`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Apply luma denoising

- **`r_luma_denoise`** (int, range: 1-4, default: `1`)
  - Purpose: Luma noise reduction level

- **`r_set_auto_exp_region`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Use ROI for exposure metering

- **`r_auto_exp_region_start_x`** (int, default: `0`)
  - Purpose: Exposure ROI start X coordinate

- **`r_auto_exp_region_start_y`** (int, default: `0`)
  - Purpose: Exposure ROI start Y coordinate

- **`r_auto_exp_region_width`** (int, default: `0`)
  - Purpose: Exposure ROI width

- **`r_auto_exp_region_height`** (int, default: `0`)
  - Purpose: Exposure ROI height

## Stereo Parameters

- **`i_fps`** (float, default: `30.0`)
  - Purpose: Stereo computation frame rate

- **`i_aligned`** (bool, default: `true`)
  - Purpose: Align depth to specified board socket

- **`i_board_socket_id`** (int, default: `0`)
  - Purpose: Socket ID for alignment target

- **`i_output_disparity`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Output disparity instead of depth

- **`i_enable_left_spatial_nn`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Enable spatial NN with left as RGB source

- **`i_enable_right_spatial_nn`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Enable spatial NN with right as RGB source

- **`i_enable_left_rgbd`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Enable RGBD with left as RGB source

- **`i_enable_right_rgbd`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Enable RGBD with right as RGB source

- **`i_run_align_on_host`** (bool, default: `true`)
  - Purpose: Run alignment on host (RVC4 only)

- **`i_depth_preset`** (string, default: `FAST_ACCURACY`)
  - Purpose: "What kind of default depth preset to apply"
  - Options: `FAST_ACCURACY`, `DEFAULT`, `FACE`, `HIGH_DETAIL`, `ROBOTICS`

- **`i_enable_distortion_correction`** (bool, default: `true`)
  - Purpose: Toggle distortion correction on output

- **`i_bilateral_sigma`** (int, range: 0-65535, default: `0`)
  - Purpose: Bilateral filter intensity parameter

- **`i_lrc_threshold`** (int, range: 0-255, default: `10`)
  - Purpose: Left-right consistency threshold

- **`i_stereo_conf_threshold`** (int, range: 0-255, default: `15`)
  - Purpose: Disparity confidence threshold

- **`i_subpixel`** (bool, default: `true`)
  - Purpose: Enable sub-pixel disparity computation

- **`i_subpixel_fractional_bits`** (int, range: 3-5, default: `3`)
  - Purpose: Fractional disparity bits (2^x)

- **`i_extended_disp`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Extend disparity range 0-190 for short range

- **`i_enable_temporal_filter`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Apply temporal filtering

- **`i_temporal_filter_alpha`** (float, range: 0.0-1.0, default: `0.4`)
  - Purpose: "The alpha factor in an exponential moving average"

- **`i_temporal_filter_delta`** (int, default: `20`)
  - Purpose: Temporal edge preservation threshold

- **`i_temporal_filter_persistency`** (string, default: `VALID_2_IN_LAST_4`)
  - Purpose: Invalid value replacement strategy
  - Options: `PERSISTENCY_OFF`, `VALID_8_OUT_OF_8`, `VALID_2_IN_LAST_3`, `VALID_2_IN_LAST_4`, `VALID_2_OUT_OF_8`, `VALID_1_IN_LAST_2`, `VALID_1_IN_LAST_5`, `VALID_1_IN_LAST_8`, `PERSISTENCY_INDEFINITELY`

- **`i_enable_spatial_filter`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Apply spatial filtering

- **`i_spatial_filter_hole_filling_radius`** (int, default: `2`)
  - Purpose: Hole-filling search radius

- **`i_spatial_filter_alpha`** (float, default: `0.5`)
  - Purpose: Spatial smoothing factor

- **`i_spatial_filter_delta`** (int, default: `20`)
  - Purpose: Spatial edge preservation threshold

- **`i_spatial_filter_num_iterations`** (int, default: `1`)
  - Purpose: Filter pass iterations

- **`i_enable_speckle_filter`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Apply speckle removal filtering

- **`i_speckle_filter_speckle_range`** (int, default: `50`)
  - Purpose: Speckle search range

- **`i_enable_disparity_shift`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Shift disparity to increase minimum depth

- **`i_disparity_shift`** (int, default: `0`)
  - Purpose: Pixel shift amount

- **`i_enable_threshold_filter`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Apply depth range threshold

- **`i_threshold_filter_min_range`** (int, default: `400`)
  - Purpose: Minimum depth in mm

- **`i_threshold_filter_max_range`** (int, default: `15000`)
  - Purpose: Maximum depth in mm

- **`i_enable_brightness_filter`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Filter by pixel brightness

- **`i_brightness_filter_min_brightness`** (int, default: `0`)
  - Purpose: Minimum brightness threshold

- **`i_brightness_filter_max_brightness`** (int, default: `255`)
  - Purpose: Maximum brightness threshold

- **`i_enable_decimation_filter`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Reduce resolution via decimation

- **`i_decimation_filter_decimation_mode`** (string, default: `PIXEL_SKIPPING`)
  - Purpose: Decimation algorithm
  - Options: `PIXEL_SKIPPING`, `NON_ZERO_MEDIAN`, `NON_ZERO_MEAN`

- **`i_decimation_filter_decimation_factor`** (int, range: 1-4, default: `1`)
  - Purpose: Resolution reduction ratio

- **`i_use_neural_depth`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Use Neural Depth (RVC4 only)

- **`i_neural_depth_model`** (string, default: `NEURAL_DEPTH_SMALL`)
  - Purpose: Neural Depth variant
  - Options: `NEURAL_DEPTH_LARGE`, `NEURAL_DEPTH_MEDIUM`, `NEURAL_DEPTH_SMALL`, `NEURAL_DEPTH_NANO`

- **`r_edge_threshold`** (int, default: `10`)
  - Purpose: Neural Depth edge threshold

- **`r_confidence_threshold`** (int, default: `125`)
  - Purpose: Neural Depth confidence threshold

- **`i_publish_left_rect`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Publish rectified left stream

- **`i_publish_right_rect`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Publish rectified right stream

## Neural Network Parameters

- **`i_nn_family`** (string, default: `detection`)
  - Purpose: Neural network task type
  - Options: `detection`, `segmentation`

- **`i_nn_model`** (string, default: `yolov6-nano`)
  - Purpose: Model identifier for automatic download

- **`i_nn_confidence_threshold`** (float, default: `0.5`)
  - Purpose: Detection confidence minimum

- **`i_nn_depth_lower_threshold`** (int, default: `100`)
  - Purpose: Spatial NN minimum depth (mm)

- **`i_nn_depth_upper_threshold`** (int, default: `10000`)
  - Purpose: Spatial NN maximum depth (mm)

- **`i_enable_passthrough`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Publish RGB input image

- **`i_enable_passthrough_depth`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Publish depth input image

- **`i_bounding_box_scale_factor`** (float, range: 0.0-1.0, default: `0.5`)
  - Purpose: Detection box scaling factor

- **`i_max_q_size`** (int, default: `8`)
  - Purpose: NN output queue size

## Feature Tracker Parameters

- **`i_enable_feature_tracker`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Enable tracker for sensor

- **`i_motion_estimation_type`** (string, default: `LUKAS_KANADE_OPTICAL_FLOW`)
  - Purpose: Motion computation method
  - Options: `LUKAS_KANADE_OPTICAL_FLOW`, `HW_MOTION_ESTIMATION`

## IMU Parameters

- **`i_max_q_size`** (int, default: `8`)
  - Purpose: IMU queue size

- **`i_acc_freq`** (int, default: `480`)
  - Purpose: Accelerometer sampling frequency (Hz)

- **`i_acc_cov`** (float, default: `0.0`)
  - Purpose: Accelerometer measurement covariance

- **`i_batch_report_threshold`** (int, default: `5`)
  - Purpose: Report batch size threshold

- **`i_enable_rotation`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Enable rotation vector and magnetometer

- **`i_gyro_cov`** (float, default: `0.0`)
  - Purpose: Gyroscope measurement covariance

- **`i_gyro_freq`** (int, default: `400`)
  - Purpose: Gyroscope sampling frequency (Hz)

- **`i_mag_cov`** (float, default: `0.0`)
  - Purpose: Magnetometer measurement covariance

- **`i_mag_freq`** (int, default: `100`)
  - Purpose: Magnetometer sampling frequency (Hz)

- **`i_max_batch_reports`** (int, default: `10`)
  - Purpose: Maximum reports per batch

- **`i_message_type`** (string, default: `IMU`)
  - Purpose: ROS publisher message format
  - Options: `IMU`, `IMU_WITH_MAG`, `IMU_WITH_MAG_SPLIT`

- **`i_rot_cov`** (float, default: `-1.0`)
  - Purpose: Rotation vector measurement covariance

- **`i_rot_freq`** (int, default: `400`)
  - Purpose: Rotation vector sampling frequency (Hz)

- **`i_sync_method`** (string, default: `COPY`)
  - Purpose: IMU data synchronization approach
  - Options: `COPY`, `LINEAR_INTERPOLATE_GYRO`, `LINEAR_INTERPOLATE_ACCEL`

- **`i_rotation_vector_type`** (string)
  - Purpose: Rotation representation format
  - Options: `ROTATION_VECTOR`, `GAME_ROTATION_VECTOR`, `GEOMAGNETIC_ROTATION_VECTOR`, `ARVR_STABILIZED_ROTATION_VECTOR`, `ARVR_STABILIZED_GAME_ROTATION_VECTOR`

## Sync Parameters

- **`i_enable_sync`** (bool, default: `false`)
  - Purpose: Activate synchronized message group

- **`i_sync_threshold`** (int, default: `10`)
  - Purpose: Maximum inter-message interval (ms)

- **`i_sync_attempts`** (int, default: `10`)
  - Purpose: Synchronization attempt count

## ToF Parameters

- **`i_publish_topic`** (bool, default: `true`)

## Thermal Parameters

- **`r_auto_ffc`** (bool, default: `false`)

## RGBD Parameters

# OAK-D-Lite CPU Issue Summary

## Problem
A user running ROS 2 Jazzy on a Turtlebot4 reported that after approximately 23 hours of operation, the OAK-D-Lite camera driver began consuming 100% CPU and stopped publishing image data, despite initially using only 3-5% of one CPU core.

## Key Details
- **System**: Raspberry Pi 5 (8GB), Ubuntu 24.04
- **Package**: depthai-ros-driver v2.10.3-1
- **Resolution**: Restarting the turtlebot4 service restored normal operation (~2% CPU usage, 30Hz publishing)
- **Impact**: Power consumption increased from 5.4W to 6.8W during the failure

## Discussion Status
The provided content shows **only the initial issue report** with no comments, workarounds, or maintainer responses visible. No discussion of parameters like `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error`, `i_enable_diagnostics`, USB speed configurations, `max_q_size`, or watchdog mechanisms appears in this excerpt.

## Recommendation
To find potential solutions, you would need to review subsequent comments on issue #657 or check related pull requests and closed issues in the depthai-ros repository for community-discovered workarounds or official fixes.

content.content:
# Parameter Declarations Summary

Based on the source code, here are the exact `declareAndLogParam` calls for your requested parameters:

## Common Parameters (all sensors)
- `i_fps`: default `30.0`
- `i_max_q_size`: default `30`
- `i_low_bandwidth`: default `false`
- `i_low_bandwidth_quality`: default `50`
- `i_enable_lazy_publisher`: default `true`
- `i_publish_compressed`: default `false`
- `i_get_base_device_timestamp`: default `false`

## Mono Camera Specific
- `i_publish_topic`: default `publish` (parameter-dependent)
- `i_resolution`: default `sensor.defaultResolution`
- `i_width`: declares post-resolution value via `monoCam->getResolutionWidth()`
- `i_height`: declares post-resolution value via `monoCam->getResolutionHeight()`

## Color Camera Specific
- `i_publish_topic`: default `publish` (parameter-dependent)
- `i_resolution`: default `sensor.defaultResolution`
- `i_set_isp_scale`: default `false` (or `true` if resolution is "1080P" or "1200P")
- `i_isp_num`: default `2`
- `i_isp_den`: default `3` (or `5` for "1200P")
- `i_width`: default computed from ISP scaling or resolution
- `i_height`: default computed from ISP scaling or resolution

**Note:** `i_width` and `i_height` differ between sensor types; mono declares actual resolution dimensions while color involves ISP scaling calculations.

# Device Disconnect and Auto-Recovery in DepthAI ROS Driver

Based on the provided code, the driver implements limited auto-recovery mechanisms:

## Diagnostics Callback

The `diagCB()` function monitors diagnostics messages. When it detects an error status matching the camera node's sys_logger, it logs the error and checks a parameter:

> "if(ph->getParam<bool>("i_restart_on_diagnostics_error")) { restart(); }"

This means diagnostics-driven recovery is **conditionally gated** by the `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error` parameter.

## Restart Logic

The `restart()` function performs a sequential stop-then-start cycle:

> "stop(); start(); if(camRunning) { return; } else { RCLCPP_ERROR(get_logger(), "Restarting camera failed."); }"

Recovery succeeds silently or logs failure if the device doesn't resume.

## Missing Components

The provided code snippet **lacks**:
- A dedicated device-disconnect callback or watchdog timer
- An `i_enable_diagnostics` parameter definition (only `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error` is visible)
- A `closedStateCb` or `restartFromBoot` function
- Proactive monitoring for USB/network disconnection

## Conclusion

Recovery appears **reactive rather than proactive**—it relies on external diagnostic messages rather than internal device state monitoring. Without additional code showing watchdog initialization or disconnect callbacks, this driver depends on the diagnostics system to detect and trigger restarts.

This requires `i_enable_diagnostics: true` (the default in v2 humble). Let me confirm one detail about the diagnostics node and the v2 README recommendations for low-resource/Pi setups.

content.text:
I have all the authoritative data I need from the source files. Here is the final research report.

---

# depthai_ros_driver (ros-humble-depthai ~2.29.0) — OAK-D-LITE stability & auto-recovery on a Pi 4

All defaults below are confirmed against the **`humble` branch source** (`camera_param_handler.cpp`, `sensor_param_handler.cpp`, `camera.cpp`), not the v3/RVC4 docs — the v3 docs disagree on several defaults (e.g. v3 says `i_usb_speed` defaults to `SUPER`/`SUPER_PLUS` and `i_nn_type` to `none`; the humble source actually ships `i_usb_speed=SUPER` and `i_nn_type=spatial`). For the 2.x line you SSH into, **trust the source-derived values in the table.**

## The one thing that matters most for auto-recovery

The driver's **only** built-in self-healing path is diagnostics-gated and is **OFF by default**:

- A `SystemLogger` (diagnostics) node publishes device health. This node only exists when **`camera.i_enable_diagnostics: true`** (default `true` in humble).
- `Camera::diagCB()` watches diagnostics for an **error** status on the camera node's `sys_logger`. On error, **if `camera.i_restart_on_diagnostics_error: true`**, it calls `Camera::restart()`.
- `restart()` does `stop(); start();` — it **tears down and re-initializes the device**, which is exactly the "don't sit dead after a disconnect" behavior you want. If it can't come back it logs `"Restarting camera failed."`

There is **no separate USB-disconnect callback and no watchdog timer** in the humble driver. Recovery is **reactive** via diagnostics. So the recovery recipe is: keep `i_enable_diagnostics: true` **and** set `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error: true`. (Your current bring-up does not set the latter, so the camera currently "sits dead" on a stall — this matches gotcha #8 in CLAUDE.md, the X_LINK stall.)

Caveat from the field: GitHub issue #657 (OAK-D-LITE on a TB4/Pi-class host) shows the node climbing to 100% CPU and going to 0 Hz after long runs; the diagnostics-restart can recover *device* errors, but a host-side X_LINK/CPU wedge may not always raise a diagnostics *error* — so keep your PID-based external relaunch (gotcha #8) as the backstop.

## Parameter table

| Param (prefix `camera.` unless noted) | Purpose | Type | Default (humble) | Recommended for Pi 4 | Why |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error` | Auto stop+start (re-init device) when diagnostics reports an error | bool | `false` | **`true`** | The single switch that makes the node self-recover instead of sitting dead. |
| `i_enable_diagnostics` | Spawns the SystemLogger diagnostics node that feeds `diagCB()` | bool | `true` | **`true`** (keep) | Required for the restart-on-error path to fire at all. Disabling it saves a trickle of CPU but kills auto-recovery. |
| `i_usb_speed` | USB link speed | string (`LOW/FULL/HIGH/SUPER/SUPER_PLUS`) | `SUPER` | **`HIGH`** (USB2) | OAK-D-LITE **boot-loops on USB3** when power-limited (gotcha #2). Forcing `HIGH`/USB2 is the biggest single stability win on the Pi 4. |
| `i_pipeline_type` | Which DAI nodes are built | string (`RGB/RGBD/Stereo/Depth/...`) | `RGBD` | **`RGBD`** (or `Stereo`/`Depth`) | You only need depth. `RGBD`/`Stereo` avoids building NN/color paths you don't use. (You currently pass `RGBD`.) |
| `i_nn_type` | Build a neural-net node | string (`none/rgb/spatial`) | **`spatial`** | **`none`** | Default `spatial` loads a blob and burns SHAVE/host cycles. `none` removes it entirely — big CPU/USB-bandwidth saving, fewer X_LINK queues to stall. You already set this. |
| `i_enable_imu` | Build IMU node + queue | bool | `true` | **`false`** | One fewer XLink stream and host callback; you don't use IMU for mapping. You already set this. |
| `i_enable_sync` | Host-side message sync group | bool | `true` | `false` if not aligning streams | Sync adds a host buffer/queue; unneeded for raw depth-only. Minor saving. |
| `i_enable_ir` | IR laser/floodlight (no-op on LITE) | bool | `true` | leave | OAK-D-LITE has no IR projector; harmless. |
| `i_device_id` / `i_mx_id` | Bind to a specific device by serial | string | `""` | set to the unit's MXID | On a multi-device or flaky-enumeration host, pinning the MXID prevents grabbing the wrong/half-enumerated device on restart — makes auto-recovery deterministic. |
| `i_ip` | Connect to a PoE device by IP | string | `""` | leave empty | USB device; N/A. |
| `i_usb_port_id` | Bind to a specific USB port path | string | `""` | optional | Like MXID, pins enumeration across replugs/restarts. |
| `i_pipeline_dump` / `i_calibration_dump` | Debug JSON dumps | bool | `false` | `false` | Diagnostics only. |
| **stereo / left / right / rgb** | | | | | |
| `left.i_fps` / `right.i_fps` (and `stereo.i_fps`) | Sensor frame rate | float | `30.0` | **`6.0`** (you do this) | Lower FPS = less SHAVE/host load and USB bandwidth → far fewer stalls. Mapping doesn't need 30 Hz depth. |
| `*.i_resolution` | Mono sensor resolution | string | sensor default (`720`/`800` class) | **`400`** (`THE_400_P`) | 400p depth roughly halves pixel throughput vs 720/800p; you already pass `stereo.i_resolution:=400`. Lower res = lower USB bandwidth = more stable on USB2. |
| `left/right.i_max_q_size`, `stereo.i_max_q_size` | DAI/host queue depth per stream | int | `30` | **`2`–`4`** | The `30`-deep default lets frames pile up when the Pi falls behind, spiking RAM/latency and masking stalls. A shallow queue (`2`) drops stale frames and keeps latency bounded — more graceful degradation. |
| `i_low_bandwidth` (+ `i_low_bandwidth_quality`, default `50`) | On-device MJPEG/H26x encode to shrink the USB payload | bool / int | `false` / `50` | **`true`** for color streams; **leave `false` for the depth stream** | Cuts USB2 bandwidth pressure dramatically *for image streams* and reduces stall risk. Note it **encodes**, so don't apply it to the 16-bit depth map you fuse — only to RGB/mono if you publish them. |
| `rgb.i_publish_topic` | Publish color | bool | `true` for CAM_A | **`false`** | You don't use RGB for fusion; killing it removes an XLink stream and host publisher. You already set `rgb.i_publish_topic:=false`. |
| `left.i_publish_topic` / `right.i_publish_topic` | Publish rectified mono | bool | `false` (non-CAM_A) | `false` | Keep off; only depth is needed downstream. |
| `stereo.i_publish_topic` | Publish the depth image | bool | `true` | **`true`** (keep) | This is your one needed output. |
| `stereo.i_align_depth` | Align depth to RGB (extra warp) | bool | `true`-ish | **`false`** | You fuse raw depth; alignment adds host/SHAVE work for nothing. You already set `stereo.i_align_depth:=false`. |
| `*.i_enable_lazy_publisher` | Only run/publish a stream when something subscribes | bool | `true` | **`true`** (keep) | If your fusion node ever drops its subscription, lazy-publish lets the camera idle that stream instead of churning — less wasted CPU. |
| `*.i_publish_compressed` | Also emit `compressed` image transport | bool | `false` | `false` | Extra encode/CPU; unneeded. |
| `*.i_get_base_device_timestamp` | Host-clock timestamping | bool | `false` | leave `false` | Minor. |
| `rgb.i_set_isp_scale` (+ `i_isp_num`/`i_isp_den`, `2`/`3`) | Downscale color on ISP | bool | `false` (true for 1080/1200p) | n/a if RGB off | Only relevant if you publish RGB; downscaling there cuts bandwidth. |

> Naming note: ROS 2 uses dots (`camera.i_usb_speed`); `i_*` params are **init-time** — to change them you must call the `stop_camera`/`start_camera` services (or relaunch). `r_*` params are runtime-settable.

## Most stable + auto-recovering bring-up (recommended combo)

Build on your current command and add the two recovery flags plus shallow queues:

```bash
ros2 run depthai_ros_driver camera_node --ros-args \
  -r __ns:=/turtlebot468 -r __node:=oakd \
  -p camera.i_pipeline_type:=RGBD \
  -p camera.i_usb_speed:=HIGH \            # USB2 — stops the USB3 boot-loop
  -p camera.i_nn_type:=none \              # no NN node → big CPU/bandwidth cut
  -p camera.i_enable_imu:=false \          # one fewer XLink stream
  -p camera.i_enable_diagnostics:=true \   # REQUIRED for auto-restart (default true)
  -p camera.i_restart_on_diagnostics_error:=true \   # <-- self-heal on device error
  -p camera.i_device_id:=<MXID> \          # pin the device so restart re-grabs the right one
  -p rgb.i_publish_topic:=false -p rgb.i_enable_preview:=false \
  -p left.i_fps:=6.0 -p right.i_fps:=6.0 \
  -p left.i_max_q_size:=2 -p right.i_max_q_size:=2 -p stereo.i_max_q_size:=2 \
  -p stereo.i_publish_topic:=true -p stereo.i_align_depth:=false -p stereo.i_resolution:=400
```

Rationale for the combination:
- **`i_usb_speed:=HIGH`** is the dominant stability fix on a power-constrained Pi 4 — it removes the USB3 brown-out boot-loop entirely.
- **`i_restart_on_diagnostics_error:=true` + `i_enable_diagnostics:=true`** is the only in-driver auto-recovery: a device error triggers `restart()` (stop→start, full device re-init) instead of the node sitting at 0 Hz.
- **`i_nn_type:=none` + `i_enable_imu:=false` + RGB off + 6 fps + 400p + shallow `i_max_q_size`** collectively minimize SHAVE/host CPU and USB2 bandwidth. On a compute-bound Pi 4 (CLAUDE.md gotcha #4) this is what keeps the camera from being starved into an X_LINK stall in the first place — the cheapest stall is the one that never happens.
- **Pin `i_device_id` (MXID)** so that after a `restart()` (or a manual replug) the driver re-attaches to the intended unit deterministically.
- **Keep an external PID-based relaunch** (CLAUDE.md gotcha #8) as the backstop: the diagnostics-restart recovers *device-reported* errors, but a host-side CPU wedge / silent X_LINK hang may not raise a diagnostics error, and the humble driver has no watchdog timer to catch that case.
